feedback_addressed: Added comprehensive interest rate sensitivity analysis: per-stock PE impact at 4.5%, 5.0%, and 5.5% 10-year yields with price impact percentages
Added NEE as SELL recommendation: most expensive large-cap utility at 25.3x with least near-term data center leverage and behind-the-meter competition risk
Separated contracted vs speculative demand with methodology: signed ESAs vs interconnection queue requests
Added dividend sustainability analysis for every pick: payout ratios, dividend coverage, FCF coverage, growth rates, sustainability ratings
Added behind-the-meter generation risk: 25 GW pipeline over 5 years, Google $4.75B Intersect Power acquisition signaling hyperscaler vertical integration
Added interconnection queue reality: 8+ years from application to commercial operation, severely constraining speculative demand conversion
Flagged AEP FCF yield of -7.2% and equity dilution of 2-4% annually from continuous issuance
Added natural gas price ceiling on nuclear PPAs: if gas stays below $4.50/MMBtu, nuclear PPAs above $60/MWh face renegotiation risk
feedback_ignored: ETR 11.5% EPS growth math (rate base assumptions, allowed ROE, regulatory lag factors) still not shown with explicit calculation
remaining_flaws: The report still has 5-6 BUY ratings plus HOLD ratings in a sector acknowledged to be 7% above fair value -- the NEE SELL is good but more HOLDs or SELLs would better reflect the overvaluation
VST at 2.8x debt-to-equity rated as highest conviction despite the leverage concerns flagged in v1 -- the debt analysis is improved but the rating seems aggressive
Vogtle cost overrun risk at SO is mentioned but the specific dollar amount of remaining construction debt and amortization timeline is not quantified
CEG Calpine acquisition analysis lacks the specific revenue/EBITDA multiple comparison to other power sector M&A that v1 demanded
what_impressed: Interest rate sensitivity table with specific per-stock price impact at 3 yield scenarios (NEE -22% at 5.5%, DUK -24%) is the most critical analysis for utility investors and was completely absent in v1
Behind-the-meter generation risk quantification (25 GW, Google Intersect Power acquisition) directly challenges the all-demand-flows-through-grid thesis
Dividend sustainability ratings (STRONG for CEG/VST, MODERATE for NEE/DUK/SO, WATCH for AEP/ETR) with FCF coverage metrics give income investors actionable data
NEE SELL recommendation: calling the most popular renewable utility overvalued at 25.3x when its hyperscaler customers are moving to baseload nuclear/gas is genuinely contrarian
Contracted vs speculative demand distinction (30-40 GW signed ESAs vs 200+ GW queue requests) is the single most important analytical framework for utility AI-demand investing
Interconnection queue reality (8+ year timelines) tempering the speculative demand narrative shows evidence-based skepticism
